nodes.label: .graphifyignore Junk Exclusion
nodes.rationale: graphify indexes everything not ignored, so backups/vendored/minified/compiled/generated code pollute the graph with garbage communities unless excluded before the first build.

nodes.label: Parallel AST Extraction Crash on macOS
nodes.rationale: graphify's parallel AST extraction can crash on macOS ('process pool terminated abruptly', yields 0 edges), so the full graph must be built serially with parallel=False; incremental update is safe under ~20 changed files.
nodes.norm_label: parallel ast extraction crash on macos

nodes.label: GRAPHIFY_NO_BACKUP Environment Flag
nodes.rationale: graphify auto-creates a dated backup folder before overwrites once labels are curated; setting GRAPHIFY_NO_BACKUP=1 keeps graphify-out/ clean without dated snapshot dirs.

nodes.label: Effortless Delightful Input
nodes.rationale: People quit finance apps because keeping them current is annoying; if input is painless, users stay — this is the retention engine.

nodes.label: Spending Detective
nodes.rationale: Deterministic period-over-period attribution: computes deltas per category/merchant and ranks drivers, valuing the 'why' over the 'what', with no AI/ML/LLM involved in V1.

nodes.label: Safe-to-Spend Number
nodes.rationale: The one hero number a user must trust; it is the daily reason to open the app, so it must feel right or the product loses trust.

nodes.label: Normal / Baseline Spending
nodes.rationale: Derived from history (e.g. median of last 3 months) rather than requiring a manual budget, powering the dashboard, budgeting, and Detective.

nodes.label: Transfer Transaction Type
nodes.rationale: Moving money between own accounts must never count as expense or income; getting this wrong makes every total wrong.

nodes.label: Refund Transaction Type
nodes.rationale: Money back for a prior purchase reduces spend in its category; it is not income.

nodes.label: Recurring Series Detection
nodes.rationale: Detected by pattern (merchant + amount + interval) then confirmed by the user — never auto-committed silently.

nodes.label: Categorization Rules Engine
nodes.rationale: Since there is no AI, categorization = shipped merchant dictionary + fuzzy string match + user-taught rules; deterministic and improves per user via corrections.

nodes.label: No Pandas in Request Path (SQL-first analytics)
nodes.rationale: Postgres does aggregations better and faster than a pandas dataframe cold-started on every serverless call; keeps deploy bundle tiny for fast cold starts and analytics correctness in one source of truth.
nodes.norm_label: no pandas in request path (sql-first analytics)

nodes.label: Row-Level Security (RLS)
nodes.rationale: Mandatory security requirement: every user-scoped table enforces auth.uid() = user_id, with app filter + RLS as two independent layers, since a user must never read another user's row.

nodes.label: Keyboard-First Quick-Add Bar
nodes.rationale: Data entry is the single biggest churn driver in this app category; sub-5-second keyboard-only entry with a live preview is the retention feature.

nodes.label: Create-on-the-Fly Category Picker
nodes.rationale: Category friction is where tracking dies; instant, forgiving category creation keeps data clean, which makes every downstream insight correct.

nodes.label: Bank CSV Import Wizard
nodes.rationale: Import is both onboarding and ongoing top-up; a smooth first import and one-click second import make the app effortless to keep current.

nodes.label: Chunked Import Commit
nodes.rationale: Imports must be chunked (e.g. 200 rows per request) so no single request exceeds Vercel serverless execution-time limits.
